sections.paragraph:
ἀετός, οῦ, ὁ [etym. complex, cp. Lat. avis ‘bird’] ‘large bird of the family Accipitri-dae’

—a. as bird of prey and majestic symbol eagle Rv 4:7; 8:13; 12:14.

—b. with focus on carrion vulture Mt 24:28; Lk 17:37. A case can be made for metaphorical association of ἀετός in these two last passages with Roman military standards, but the focus remains on the jeopardy to which disciples are exposed.
